cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I'll look back at the passage. The first sentence says, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So right here, the key point is receiving her bachelor's degree. The other parts mention her son, parents being at the ceremony, and her plans for a teaching career. But the main reason given for it being a happy time is getting her degree. Option B says "she had just received her bachelor's degree." That matches the text. Option A talks about being a good wife and having two jobs. While she did have two jobs, that's mentioned as something she did while getting the degree, not the reason for happiness. Option C says she was a teacher at Ashland University, but the text says she was graduating there and was about to start her teaching career, not that she was already
